svg.title: Reconciliation Processing Architecture
svg.desc: End-to-end architecture for processing millions of RCON records from three file sources into a single PostgreSQL database

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What format should Source A flat files be in for RCON reconciliation?')
sendPrompt('What format should Source B flat files be in?')
sendPrompt('What format should Source C flat files be in?')
sendPrompt('What is a file landing zone in data architecture?')
g.text: S3 / SFTP / NFS — raw flat files (CSV, pipe-delimited, fixed-width)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why use Kafka for RCON file ingestion?')
g.text: File-arrival events, backpressure, replay on failure

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does file parsing work in RCON processing?')
sendPrompt('What validation rules are needed for RCON data?')
sendPrompt('How does the reconciliation matching engine work?')

svg.text: PostgreSQL — single database, partitioned by date + entity

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How to design a staging table in PostgreSQL for reconciliation?')
sendPrompt('How to design a reconciliation results table in PostgreSQL?')
sendPrompt('How to design an audit and error log table in PostgreSQL?')
sendPrompt('How to build a reconciliation analyst dashboard?')
sendPrompt('How to generate RCON regulatory reports automatically?')
sendPrompt('How to set up break alerts and exception management in RCON?')
sendPrompt('What should I monitor in a reconciliation pipeline?')
g.text: File lag · Row counts · Match rate · DB query time · Error rate

svg.text: Click any component to learn more
